title.en: REST API Concepts: Building Web Services
title.ta: REST API கருத்துகள்: வலை சேவைகளை உருவாக்குதல்

description.en: Learn REST principles, how to design endpoints, perform CRUD operations, and work with JSON data.
description.ta: REST கொள்கைகள், எண்ட்பாயிண்ட்கள் வடிவமைப்பு, CRUD செயல்பாடுகள் மற்றும் JSON தரவுடன் வேலை செய்வதைக் கற்றுக்கொள்ளுங்கள்.

story_variants.en:
## The Restaurant Menu of APIs

Vaathiyaar draws a restaurant on the whiteboard. "Imagine an API is a restaurant. The **menu** lists what you can order (endpoints), the **waiter** carries your order to the kitchen (HTTP), and the **kitchen** prepares the food (server logic). REST is a set of rules that makes this process efficient and predictable."

### REST Principles
- **Stateless** — Each request contains all info needed
- **Resource-based** — URLs represent resources (nouns, not verbs)
- **Uniform interface** — Consistent URL patterns
- **Client-Server** — Separation of concerns

### CRUD Mapping

| Operation | HTTP Method | Endpoint | Example |
|-----------|------------|----------|----------|
| **C**reate | POST | `/api/users` | Add a new user |
| **R**ead | GET | `/api/users/1` | Get user #1 |
| **U**pdate | PUT/PATCH | `/api/users/1` | Update user #1 |
| **D**elete | DELETE | `/api/users/1` | Remove user #1 |

### JSON: The Language of REST

```json
{
  "id": 1,
  "name": "Alice",
  "email": "alice@example.com",
  "is_active": true
}
```

> **Key Concept:** REST is not a protocol — it's an architectural style. Any API that follows REST principles is called a RESTful API.

explanation.en: Import requests to interact with REST APIs
Import json for pretty-printing JSON responses
Define the base URL — a free fake REST API for testing
The C in CRUD: Create a new resource on the server
Prepare the data as a Python dictionary
POST sends data to create a new resource at /posts
201 means the resource was successfully created
The R in CRUD: Retrieve an existing resource
GET /posts/1 retrieves the post with id=1
Parse the JSON response into a Python dict
Access the title field from the response

steps.line: # UPDATE — PUT replaces the entire resource

explanation.en: The U in CRUD: Update an existing resource
PUT requires sending the complete updated resource
PUT /posts/1 replaces the entire post with new data
200 means the resource was successfully updated
The D in CRUD: Delete an existing resource
DELETE /posts/1 removes the post with id=1

output: Title: sunt aut facere repellat provident occaecati excepturi optio reprehenderit

animation_sequence.title: REST CRUD Operations
animation_sequence.code:
import requests
BASE = 'https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com'

resp = requests.post(f'{BASE}/posts', json={'title': 'Hello', 'body': 'World', 'userId': 1})
print(f'CREATE: {resp.status_code}')
resp = requests.get(f'{BASE}/posts/1')
print(f'READ: {resp.status_code}')
resp = requests.put(f'{BASE}/posts/1', json={'title': 'Updated'})
print(f'UPDATE: {resp.status_code}')
resp = requests.delete(f'{BASE}/posts/1')
print(f'DELETE: {resp.status_code}')

instruction.en: Use the requests library to perform all four CRUD operations against jsonplaceholder.typicode.com. Create a new post, read it, update its title, then delete it. Print the status code after each operation.
instruction.ta: jsonplaceholder.typicode.com மீது நான்கு CRUD செயல்பாடுகளையும் செய்யுங்கள். புதிய இடுகையை உருவாக்குங்கள், படியுங்கள், தலைப்பை புதுப்பியுங்கள், பின் நீக்குங்கள்.

practice_challenges.starter_code:
import requests

BASE = 'https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com'

# 1. CREATE a new post
new_post = {'title': 'My Post', 'body': 'REST is cool', 'userId': 1}
create_resp = requests.post(f'{BASE}/posts', json=new_post)
print(f'CREATE: {create_resp.status_code}')

# 2. READ post with id=1
read_resp = requests.get(f'{BASE}/posts/1')
print(f'READ: {read_resp.status_code} - {read_resp.json()["title"][:30]}')

# 3. UPDATE post with id=1
updated = {'title': 'Updated Post', 'body': 'Changed!', 'userId': 1}
update_resp = requests.put(f'{BASE}/posts/1', json=updated)
print(f'UPDATE: {update_resp.status_code}')

# 4. DELETE post with id=1
delete_resp = requests.delete(f'{BASE}/posts/1')
print(f'DELETE: {delete_resp.status_code}')
practice_challenges.expected_output:
CREATE: 201
READ: 200 - sunt aut facere repellat prov
UPDATE: 200
DELETE: 200

en: POST creates new resources — use requests.post()
PUT replaces a resource — use requests.put()
DELETE removes a resource — use requests.delete()
ta: POST புதிய வளங்களை உருவாக்குகிறது — requests.post() பயன்படுத்துங்கள்

q.en: Which HTTP method should you use to create a new resource?
q.ta: புதிய வளத்தை உருவாக்க எந்த HTTP முறையைப் பயன்படுத்த வேண்டும்?

q.en: In REST, what does the URL '/api/users/5' typically represent?
